rules.statement: Every active reporting rule in the audit rule registry MUST have a corresponding entry in .intent/enforcement/remediation/auto_remediation.yaml. Absence is not a valid signal and produces a silent abandoned-finding re-emission loop.
rules.rationale: ADR-066. Without a remediation-map entry, ViolationRemediatorWorker silently skips findings via the release_unmappable path; findings age to abandoned within one TTL cycle; blackboard_query_service.py:103 excludes abandoned from the active set by design; the sensor re-emits the finding on the next cycle; the loop runs indefinitely. Peak observed: 4,282 abandoned findings for a single rule (architecture.cli.api_only) in a 48h window. The ADR-038 circuit-breaker does not fire because no proposals are ever created. Scope is reporting rules only — blocking rules are caught at IntentGuard before they reach the audit board and follow a different lifecycle. A DELEGATE or PENDING entry is sufficient to satisfy the invariant; the entry's existence is what closes the loop.
